what_you_do: I help burned-out corporate marketers replace their salary with a freelance client base so they can quit the 9-to-5 without gambling their mortgage.
best_client: Sarah, a 38-year-old marketing manager at a SaaS company. Great at her craft, terrified to go out on her own. Within 90 days she landed three retainer clients at $2k/mo each and gave notice.
result: 90 days in, they have 2-4 paying retainer clients and a repeatable way to get more, so quitting stops being a leap of faith and becomes a math problem.
process: First we pick one niche and one offer. Then we build a one-page pitch and a warm-outreach list of 50. Then we run daily outreach with scripts until the first client says yes, and systematize from there.
differentiator: I spent 12 years inside marketing departments, so I teach them to sell the exact way corporate buyers actually buy — not the guru DM-spam playbook.
story: I got laid off in 2019 with a kid on the way and no plan. I white-knuckled my way to my first $5k month, then reverse-engineered what actually worked. I do this so nobody else has to white-knuckle it.
pricing: Currently no set price, done a few at $1500. Want to sell a $3k 90-day program.
stuck_point: No repeatable way to find clients. It's all referrals and luck, and confidence dips every time a month is slow.
client_language: “I know I'm good at this, I just don't know how to get people to pay me for it without feeling gross.”
failed_solutions: Cold DM courses, a $2k mastermind that was all mindset and no system, and posting on LinkedIn hoping clients would appear.
objections: “I'm not ready.” “What if I can't get clients?” “Is this just another course?” “I don't have time while working full-time.”
